purpose: Conservative host-side limits for first Petoi Bittle X V2 bring-up.
notes: These are first-day host-side limits, not final measured Bittle joint limits.
The first-day neutral pose is the first accepted OpenCat hold pose measured on the Bittle X V2 after RL_SET_TARGETS bring-up, not the simulation residual-policy neutral pose.
The neutral target uses half-degree values so OpenCat's integer currentAng truncation does not drift by one degree on repeated no-op commands.
Servo ID, sign, zero offset, and final min/max angle must be filled after OpenCat/Bittle mapping is verified.
Do not run the learned walking policy until neutral, single-joint, and low-amplitude scripted targets are accepted by hardware.
